data.title: The leak of Warsh's testimony has sparked controversy, and the hearing may not be as captivating as usual.
data.description: The U.S. Senate Banking Committee will hold its confirmation hearing for Warsh's nomination at 10:00 AM ET on Tuesday (10:00 PM Beijing time). Foreign exchange analyst Adam Button stated that Warsh has maintained a remarkably low profile since his nomination, meaning tomorrow will be the first real opportunity for the outside world to understand his stance. The market is already familiar with Warsh before his nomination; his resume sounds hawkish. His dovish leanings lie in his belief that productivity is about to surge, therefore interest rates should be lowered. This aligns perfectly with Trump's wishes. Senators will take note of this, and he will face questioning. Politico's reporting suggests he will at least go through the motions. I don't know if anyone will believe what he says, but once Trump drops the investigation into Powell, his confirmation is essentially a done deal. Tomorrow is a hearing, not a vote. What disturbs me is that his testimony was leaked to Politico, which is terrible for a position requiring strict information control. There is only one issue that truly matters to the currency market and interest rates: independence. Will Warsh defend it, evade it, or comply? How tough will he be? Can he convince the audience without saying the things that led Trump to withdraw his nomination? Given all the drama surrounding Iran, this hearing won't be as high-profile as usual. (Jin Shi)

data.title: DeFi's Growth Through Challenges Highlighted by Dragonfly Partner
data.description:
Dragonfly partner Haseeb Qureshi recently shared insights on the X platform, emphasizing the growth of decentralized finance (DeFi) through repeated challenges. According to Odaily, Qureshi pointed out that despite setbacks such as the Terra collapse, the 2020 'Black Thursday' liquidation failure, and the 2022 stETH de-pegging incident, the industry has consistently progressed through corrections. He compared this evolution to traditional finance's development path, citing the 2008 financial crisis and the savings and loan crisis of the 1980s as examples of systems improving amid crises.

Qureshi stressed that these failures are not fatal, as DeFi's core still possesses risk control and system resilience. He highlighted Aave's ability to handle potential bad debts with sufficient capital buffers. He believes that intense discussions around improvement strategies are crucial for DeFi's ongoing evolution and reiterated his long-term optimism for DeFi and its community development.

data.title: U.S. Senate to Hold Confirmation Hearing for Walsh's Nomination
data.description:
The U.S. Senate Banking Committee is set to conduct a confirmation hearing for Walsh's nomination on Tuesday at 10 a.m. Eastern Time (UTC+8 22:00). According to Odaily, foreign exchange analyst Adam Button noted that Walsh has maintained a low profile since his nomination, making this hearing a key opportunity to understand his stance. Walsh is known for his hawkish resume, but his dovish inclination lies in his belief that productivity is set to rise, suggesting a need for lower interest rates. This aligns with U.S. President Donald Trump's preferences, which senators are likely to scrutinize.

A report from Politico suggests that Walsh's confirmation is almost assured once Trump ceases his investigation into Powell. The hearing is not a voting session, but concerns have been raised about the leak of Walsh's testimony to Politico, which is considered a significant breach for a position requiring strict information control. The primary question for the foreign exchange market and interest rates is Walsh's stance on independence. Observers are keen to see whether he will defend it, avoid it, or comply with directives. His ability to convey strength without provoking Trump to withdraw his nomination is under scrutiny, especially amid the ongoing tensions surrounding Iran. The hearing is expected to be more notable than usual due to these circumstances.

data.title: Cavaliers Lead Raptors in NBA Playoffs Amid Betting Activity
data.description:
A significant betting activity has been observed in the Polymarket prediction event for the NBA playoffs between the Cleveland Cavaliers and the Toronto Raptors. According to Odaily, an account with nearly $3 million in profits purchased $130,000 worth of bets on the Cavaliers defeating the Raptors, with an average opening price of 79¢. Additionally, the account invested $20,000 on the total score being less than 221.5, with an average opening price of 48.1¢.

The game, which began at 7 a.m. UTC+8, is currently at halftime with the Cavaliers leading the Raptors 52-45. The Cavaliers are ahead in the series with a 1-0 lead.

data.title: Dragonfly Partner: DeFi evolves through failures; the system possesses resilience and self-healing capabilities.
data.description: Haseeb Qureshi, a partner at Dragonfly, wrote on the X platform that DeFi grows through repeated failures. From the Terra crash and the liquidation failure of "Black Thursday" in 2020 to the stETH de-pegging incident in 2022, the industry has faced numerous setbacks, but each time it has progressed through correction. This process is no different from the development path of traditional finance, such as the 2008 financial crisis or the savings and loan crisis of the 1980s, where the system was perfected during crises. Haseeb emphasized that these failures are not fatal, and the core of DeFi still possesses risk control and systemic resilience. For example, even if Aave faces potential bad debts, it has sufficient capital buffers to cope. He believes that the intense discussions surrounding improvement solutions are the key mechanism driving the continuous evolution of DeFi, and reiterated his long-term optimism about the development of DeFi and its community.

data.title: C1 Fund partially exited its Ripple investment, reportedly achieving a return of approximately 150% in four months.
data.description: Crypto fund C1 Fund announced a partial exit from its investment in Ripple, achieving a return of approximately 150% in less than four months, though the exact profit amount was not disclosed. C1 Fund's portfolio also includes Kraken, Consensys, and Chainalysis, and the fund stated it will continue to invest in high-quality crypto companies. (Businesswire)

data.title: Aave Faces Potential Bad Debt Risk Due to Kelp DAO Bridge Vulnerability
data.description: Aave is confronting a potential bad debt risk following a vulnerability in the Kelp DAO cross-chain bridge that allowed approximately $190 million in loans to be issued without collateral. According to PANews, a report by Aave Labs and LlamaRisk revealed that attackers exploited the vulnerability by forging cross-chain messages to release 116,500 rsETH from the Ethereum side of the bridge. Subsequently, 89,600 rsETH were deposited into Aave as collateral, enabling the attackers to borrow around $190 million in ETH and related assets. In response, Aave swiftly froze the rsETH market and set the loan-to-value ratio to zero within hours.

data.title: Rio Tinto Reports Increased Iron Ore Production Despite Weather Challenges
data.description: Rio Tinto Group has reported a rise in iron ore production from Australia during the first quarter. Bloomberg posted on X that despite facing seasonal weather disruptions that led to port closures in March, the company managed to increase its output. The production boost comes as the mining giant navigates challenges posed by adverse weather conditions, highlighting its operational resilience.

data.title: Paradigm Unstakes Significant Amount of HYPE Tokens
data.description: On April 21, Paradigm unstaked approximately 2.14 million HYPE tokens, valued at around $88 million, according to BlockBeats. This transaction involved multiple related addresses, as reported by MLM monitoring.

data.title: U.S. President Donald Trump to Appear on CNBC Financial Program April 21
data.description: On April 21, U.S. President Donald Trump is scheduled to participate in a CNBC financial program. According to BlockBeats, the appearance will take place at 8:30 PM UTC+8. The program is expected to cover various economic topics and provide insights into current financial trends.

data.title: Senator Tillis Calls for Delay on CLARITY Market Structure Bill
data.description: Senator Thom Tillis has advised the Senate to postpone the advancement of the CLARITY market structure bill, according to Punchbowl News reporter Brendan Pedersen. Tillis emphasized the need for additional time to reach a compromise between the banking and cryptocurrency sectors concerning stablecoin interest. He indicated that the bill might progress in May.

data.title: New Zealand's First Quarter CPI Exceeds Expectations at 3.1%
data.description: New Zealand's Consumer Price Index (CPI) for the first quarter of the year recorded an annual rate of 3.1%, surpassing the anticipated 2.9%, according to Jin10. The previous quarter's CPI was also at 3.1%, indicating consistent inflationary pressures in the economy. This data suggests that inflation remains a significant concern for policymakers as they navigate economic strategies for the remainder of the year.

data.title: Apple Announces Leadership Change with John Ternus as New CEO
data.description: Apple has announced a significant leadership transition, appointing John Ternus as the new chief executive officer effective September 1. Tim Cook, the current CEO, will assume the role of executive chairman. According to NS3.AI, Ternus, who has been with Apple since 2001, has been leading the company's hardware engineering efforts since 2021. Following the announcement, Apple shares experienced a slight dip in after-hours trading, closing at $273.05.

data.title: John Oliver Criticizes Prediction Market Platforms on HBO
data.description: John Oliver, host of HBO's Last Week Tonight, recently criticized prediction market platforms Kalshi and Polymarket. According to NS3.AI, Oliver expressed his disapproval of these platforms and stated that he would not alter his behavior to affect user bets. Additionally, he raised concerns about the Commodity Futures Trading Commission's (CFTC) handling of event contracts related to terrorism, assassination, and war.

data.title: CME Reports Fed's April Rate Hike Probability at 0%
data.description: According to Jin10, the CME's FedWatch tool indicates a 0% probability of the Federal Reserve raising interest rates by 25 basis points in April. The likelihood of maintaining the current rate is 100%. Looking ahead to June, there is a 2.5% chance of a cumulative 25 basis point rate cut, while the probability of rates remaining unchanged is 97.5%.

data.title: LayerZero Infrastructure Breach Leads to rsETH Incident
data.description: Kelp has identified the cause of the rsETH incident on April 18 as a breach in LayerZero infrastructure, rather than its own systems. According to NS3.AI, the incident involved compromised RPC nodes and a DDoS attack, which allowed an attacker to forge a cross-chain message and drain assets from the bridge adapter.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | SPDR Gold Trust Holdings Decrease by 0.857 Tons
data.description: The world's largest gold ETF, SPDR Gold Trust, reported a reduction in its holdings by 0.857 tons, bringing the current total to 1059.762 tons. According to Jin10, this adjustment reflects ongoing shifts in investor sentiment and market dynamics within the precious metals sector.

data.title: Hungary's New Government Aims to Unfreeze EU Funds
data.description: Hungary's Tisza Party leader, Peter Majer, announced on April 20 that the soon-to-be-formed Hungarian government will work to unfreeze EU funds in a short period. According to Jin10, Majer stated at a press conference in Budapest that his team aims to meet the EU's key requirements within three months. He emphasized that Hungary will implement reforms in areas such as anti-corruption, media freedom, and judicial independence to align with the European Commission's expectations. Majer plans to reach a political agreement with the European Commission between May 15 and 20. He believes that the return of EU funds is crucial for stabilizing Hungary's economy and restoring investor confidence.

data.title: Playdate Catalog Bans AI-Generated Art and Music in Game Submissions
data.description: Panic has announced a ban on AI-generated art, music, and writing for game submissions to the Playdate Catalog. According to NS3.AI, while AI coding tools like GitHub Copilot are still permitted with disclosure, the upcoming Playdate Season 3 will prohibit any use of AI.

data.title: AI Dividend Proposal Aims to Address Potential Job Losses
data.description: New York Assembly member Alex Bores has introduced a proposal for an 'AI Dividend' to provide financial compensation to Americans in the event of job losses due to automation. According to NS3.AI, the framework is based on projections that suggest up to 50% of jobs could be automated in the coming years. The proposal would be triggered by indicators such as declining labor participation, wage compression, or productivity increases without corresponding job growth. However, the specifics regarding the amount and frequency of payments remain unspecified.
